entities.name: RNA INTERFERENCE
entities.description: A biological process where RNA molecules inhibit gene expression, used as a strategy for insect pest management

entities.name: INSECT PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing insect populations that damage crops to minimize economic loss

entities.name: INSECT PESTS
entities.description: Insects that cause significant crop losses and are the targets of various control strategies

entities.name: INSECTICIDE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The evolved ability of insect pests to survive doses of insecticides that would normally kill them

entities.name: TRANSGENIC PLANTS
entities.description: Plants that have been genetically engineered to express foreign genes for pest resistance

entities.name: INSECTICIDAL CRYSTAL PROTEIN GENES
entities.description: Genes from Bacillus thuringiensis that encode proteins toxic to specific insect orders

entities.name: BACILLUS THURINGIENSIS
entities.description: A gram-positive soil bacterium used as a source of insecticidal crystal protein genes

entities.name: LEPIDOPTERA
entities.description: An order of insects, including butterflies and moths, targeted by Bt transgenic plants

entities.name: COLEOPTERA
entities.description: An order of insects, including beetles, targeted by Bt transgenic plants

entities.name: INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An ecosystem-based strategy that focuses on long-term prevention of pests through a combination of techniques

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Pesticides derived from natural materials, including those developed using RNA interference
